title: Notarial Fees
description: Types of fees charged by a notary: notarial fee, taxes and court fees.
content:
Performing a notarial activity involves incurring fees, which include:

Notarial fee

A notary is entitled to remuneration for performing notarial activities, agreed with the parties to the activity, not exceeding the maximum notarial fee rates applicable to the given activity, as detailed in the Regulation of the Minister of Justice of 28 June 2004 on maximum notarial fee rates. The notary adds 23% VAT to the notarial fee.

Taxes
The notary is a taxpayer of the civil law transactions tax and the inheritance and donation tax, collected from taxable notarial activities.

Court fee
The notary collects a court fee for an application to enter the land and mortgage register, which is regulated in detail by the Act on Court Costs in Civil Cases.
metaTitle: Notarial Fees – Notarial Office Aleksandra Durys Garwolin
metaDescription: Information about notarial fees: notarial fee, civil law transaction taxes, inheritance and donation tax, and court fees.
